heading: Special rules for determining source
label: 863 Special rules for determining source

children.heading: Allocation under regulations
children.label: a Allocation under regulations
children.text: Items of gross income, expenses, losses, and deductions, other than those specified in sections 861(a) and 862(a), shall be allocated or apportioned to sources within or without the United States, under regulations prescribed by the Secretary. Where items of gross income are separately allocated to sources within the United States, there shall be deducted (for the purpose of computing the taxable income therefrom) the expenses, losses, and other deductions properly apportioned or allocated thereto and a ratable part of other expenses, losses, or other deductions which cannot definitely be allocated to some item or class of gross income. The remainder, if any, shall be included in full as taxable income from sources within the United States.

children.heading: Income partly from within and partly from without the United States
children.label: b Income partly from within and partly from without the United States

children.text: from services rendered partly within and partly without the United States,

from the sale or exchange of inventory property (within the meaning of section 865(i)(1)) produced (in whole or in part) by the taxpayer within and sold or exchanged without the United States, or produced (in whole or in part) by the taxpayer without and sold or exchanged within the United States, or
derived from the purchase of inventory property (within the meaning of section 865(i)(1)) within a possession of the United States and its sale or exchange within the United States,

children.heading: Source rule for certain transportation income
children.label: c Source rule for certain transportation income

children.heading: Transportation beginning and ending in the United States
children.label: 1 Transportation beginning and ending in the United States
children.text: All transportation income attributable to transportation which begins and ends in the United States shall be treated as derived from sources within the United States.

children.heading: Other transportation having United States connection
children.label: 2 Other transportation having United States connection
i
children.text: is not described in paragraph (1), and

children.label: ii
children.text: begins or ends in the United States,

children.heading: Special rule for personal service income
children.label: B Special rule for personal service income
i
children.text: begins in the United States and ends in a possession of the United States, or

children.label: ii
children.text: begins in a possession of the United States and ends in the United States.

children.label: A
children.text: the use (or hiring or leasing for use) of a vessel or aircraft, or

children.label: B
children.text: the performance of services directly related to the use of a vessel or aircraft.

children.heading: Source rules for space and certain ocean activities
children.label: d Source rules for space and certain ocean activities
A
children.text: if derived by a United States person, shall be sourced in the United States, and

children.label: B
children.text: if derived by a person other than a United States person, shall be sourced outside the United States.

children.label: i
children.text: any activity conducted in space, and

children.label: ii
children.text: any activity conducted on or under water not within the jurisdiction (as recognized by the United States) of a foreign country, possession of the United States, or the United States.

children.label: i
children.text: any activity giving rise to transportation income (as defined in section 863(c)),

children.label: ii
children.text: any activity giving rise to international communications income (as defined in subsection (e)(2)), and

children.label: iii
children.text: any activity with respect to mines, oil and gas wells, or other natural deposits to the extent within the United States or any foreign country or possession of the United States (as defined in section 638).

children.heading: United States persons
children.label: A United States persons
children.text: In the case of any United States person, 50 percent of any international communications income shall be sourced in the United States and 50 percent of such income shall be sourced outside the United States.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Except as provided in regulations or clause (ii), in the case of any person other than a United States person, any international communications income shall be sourced outside the United States.

children.heading: Special rule for income attributable to office or fixed place of business in the United States
children.label: ii Special rule for income attributable to office or fixed place of business in the United States
children.text: In the case of any person (other than a United States person) who maintains an office or other fixed place of business in the United States, any international communications income attributable to such office or other fixed place of business shall be sourced in the United States.

children.heading: Definition
children.label: 2 Definition
children.text: For purposes of this section, the term “international communications income” includes all income derived from the transmission of communications or data from the United States to any foreign country (or possession of the United States) or from any foreign country (or possession of the United States) to the United States.
